title: Heparin-Induced Thrombocytopenia (HIT) Type II in 65-Year-Old Woman Post-Operative Day 7 from Right Total Hip Arthroplasty on Subcutaneous Heparin DVT Prophylaxis: Platelet Drop from 220 to 88 (60% Reduction) + New Right Lower Extremity DVT + 4Ts Score 7 (High Probability); STOP ALL Heparin Products Including Flushes + Initiate Direct Thrombin Inhibitor Argatroban, AVOID Warfarin Until Platelet Recovery, and AVOID Platelet Transfusion
subtitle: HEPARIN-INDUCED THROMBOCYTOPENIA (HIT) Type II = immune-mediated PROTHROMBOTIC syndrome (despite low platelets) caused by IgG antibodies against PLATELET FACTOR 4 (PF4)/HEPARIN COMPLEX → activates platelets + endothelium + monocytes → thrombosis (paradoxical — 30-75% develop thrombosis WITHOUT treatment + 5-10% mortality); INCIDENCE = ~1-5% with unfractionated heparin (UFH) + 0.1-1% with low-molecular-weight heparin (LMWH); RISK FACTORS = post-cardiac surgery (highest risk + 1-3% incidence) + post-orthopedic + female + age + UFH > LMWH > fondaparinux + bovine > porcine heparin; TIMING typically 5-10 DAYS after heparin exposure (this patient POD 7); RAPID-ONSET HIT (within 24 hr) in patients with recent heparin exposure (past 100 days); DELAYED-ONSET HIT (after heparin stopped — up to 3 weeks); HIT TYPE I = non-immune + mild + transient + within 1-4 days + does not require treatment; HIT TYPE II = immune + serious + needs intervention; 4Ts SCORE = pre-test probability tool 0-8 points: (1) Thrombocytopenia — fall >50% with nadir ≥20k = 2 points; 30-50% fall = 1; <30% = 0; (2) Timing — typical 5-10 days = 2; <4 days with recent exposure = 2; <4 days no exposure = 0; >10 days = 1; (3) Thrombosis — new venous/arterial = 2; skin necrosis = 2; suspected = 1; (4) other causes — none apparent = 2; possible = 1; definite = 0; SCORE 0-3 = LOW probability (<1%; HIT unlikely; alternative diagnoses); 4-5 = INTERMEDIATE (10-30%); 6-8 = HIGH (30-80%); THIS PATIENT 4Ts = 7 = HIGH probability HIT; LABORATORY confirmation: ANTI-PF4 ELISA (sensitive ~99% but not specific ~85%; many false positives); FUNCTIONAL ASSAY (serotonin release assay SRA or heparin-induced platelet aggregation HIPA — gold standard + more specific; takes hours-days for results); SUSPECTED HIT requires IMMEDIATE action while awaiting confirmation (don't wait for confirmation if 4Ts high). MANAGEMENT: (1) IMMEDIATELY STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS = unfractionated heparin (drips + flushes + line maintenance + heparin-coated catheters) + LMWH (enoxaparin + dalteparin); EVEN HEPARIN FLUSHES contraindicated — use saline flushes; (2) START ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT immediately even before HIT confirmation if 4Ts ≥4: ARGATROBAN (direct thrombin inhibitor — synthetic + IV continuous infusion 2 mcg/kg/min titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline; renal dose adjust + caution in hepatic dysfunction — primarily hepatic clearance + lower dose 0.5-1 mcg/kg/min in liver disease; PT/INR affected so cannot guide warfarin transition by INR alone — use chromogenic factor Xa or anti-IIa) OR BIVALIRUDIN (direct thrombin inhibitor — IV + shorter half-life + preferred for cardiac surgery + renal-dose adjustment) OR FONDAPARINUX (factor Xa inhibitor — synthetic + less cross-reactivity with HIT antibodies + can be used in stable HIT after acute; SC); EMERGING DOAC alternative for stable HIT (apixaban + rivaroxaban + dabigatran) — increasingly accepted but ARGATROBAN/BIVALIRUDIN still standard for acute HIT with thrombosis; (3) AVOID WARFARIN until platelet count recovers >150k (early warfarin in HIT causes WARFARIN-INDUCED SKIN NECROSIS + microvascular thrombosis from protein C depletion + venous limb gangrene); transition to warfarin once platelets recover + overlap with argatroban × 5 days + INR target 2-3 then discontinue argatroban; (4) AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION (even with very low platelets) — counterintuitive but evidence-based; platelet transfusion in HIT can paradoxically WORSEN thrombosis by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation; only consider for life-threatening bleeding which is rare in HIT; (5) IMAGING — Doppler ultrasound for DVT + CT chest if PE suspected; CT abdomen/pelvis if abdominal symptoms; (6) DURATION of anticoagulation = at least 3 months for HIT with thrombosis (HITT) + at least 4 weeks for isolated HIT without thrombosis; (7) MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + documentation + future avoidance of heparin (lifelong avoidance for HIT antibodies typically wane but recurrence possible with re-exposure); (8) CARDIAC SURGERY in HIT patients = challenge (bivalirudin commonly used during CPB); informed consent + multidisciplinary; CONSEQUENCES of UNDETECTED HIT = paradoxical thrombosis (DVT + PE + arterial — stroke + MI + limb ischemia + skin necrosis at injection sites + adrenal hemorrhage rare); mortality 5-10% even with treatment + higher untreated

labs.v: 65 y/o female M. Chen, post-operative day 7 from RIGHT TOTAL HIP ARTHROPLASTY (THA) for severe osteoarthritis of right hip causing functional limitation + chronic pain × 5 years (failed conservative management — NSAIDs + PT + intra-articular injections + weight management); elective surgery 1 week ago. PMH: HTN (lisinopril 10 mg + amlodipine 5 mg — controlled); hyperlipidemia (atorvastatin 40 mg); type 2 diabetes (metformin 1000 mg BID + HbA1c 6.8); obesity (BMI 28); osteoarthritis severe right hip + mild left hip + chronic LBP; remote breast cancer 8 yr ago (stage 1; mastectomy + adjuvant chemo + 5-year tamoxifen completed; in remission + annual surveillance); no chronic anticoagulation. SOCIAL: married 38 yr to husband (68, retired engineer + at bedside throughout); 2 adult daughters (40, 38; one nearby + one across country); 4 grandchildren; works as elementary school teacher → retired 5 years ago; community engaged + church + gardening + grandchildren + travel; insurance excellent (Medicare + supplement); cultural background Chinese-American + bilingual home + Mandarin + English; family identifies as Buddhist + community + chaplain coordinated with Buddhist chaplain. SURGICAL HX: appendectomy childhood; mastectomy + reconstruction 8 yr ago; right TKA 6 yr ago (recovered well + no complications); right THA 7 days ago (current admission). PRIOR HEPARIN EXPOSURE: routine perioperative heparin × prior surgeries (mastectomy + TKA + appendectomy) without prior reactions; recent heparin exposure unknown >100 days. CURRENT MEDICATIONS pre-admission: lisinopril 10 mg + amlodipine 5 mg + atorvastatin 40 mg + metformin 1000 mg BID + tamoxifen completed; current admission medications: subcutaneous HEPARIN 5000 units q8h SC × 7 days (DVT prophylaxis since POD 0); oxycodone 5 mg PO PRN × 4 days then transitioned to acetaminophen + NSAIDs caution due to age + renal; lisinopril + amlodipine + atorvastatin + metformin continued; PCA hydromorphone × first 48 hr post-op then PO transition. SURGICAL COURSE: elective right THA 7 days ago — uncomplicated 2-hr procedure under spinal anesthesia with sedation; EBL 200 mL; standard DVT prophylaxis with SC heparin (some institutions use enoxaparin; this institution UFH q8h); POD 1-5 progressed well — PT bedside + early mobility + tolerating diet + minimal pain + planned discharge POD 6 or 7. POD 6 ROUTINE LABS showed platelet drop from baseline 220 (preop) → 158 (POD 4) → 112 (POD 6) — initially attributed to postoperative consumption (common in major orthopedic surgery — 10-30% drop) but trend concerning + atypical magnitude. POD 7 0700 AM LABS revealing: PLATELETS 88 (60% drop from baseline; nadir continues); Hgb 11.2 + WBC 8.4 + normal differential; PT 13.4 + INR 1.1 (normal); aPTT 32 (normal — pretreatment baseline); fibrinogen 380; LFTs normal; Cr 0.9 (normal); BUN 14; electrolytes normal. PATIENT complaining of NEW RIGHT LOWER EXTREMITY pain + swelling + warmth × 8 hr (overnight onset); RIGHT calf circumference 38 cm vs LEFT 34 cm (4 cm difference; significant); Homan's sign positive (calf pain on dorsiflexion); pitting edema 2+ right; warmth + erythema; HIP surgical site healing well + no concerning signs. DOPPLER ULTRASOUND right lower extremity STAT = ACUTE DVT in right common femoral vein + extending to popliteal vein (no extension above femoral); no left lower extremity DVT. CLINICAL SCENARIO = thrombocytopenia + new thrombosis + heparin exposure × 7 days = HIGH SUSPICION FOR HEPARIN-INDUCED THROMBOCYTOPENIA (HIT); 4Ts SCORE calculation needed + STAT hematology consultation.
labs.r: POST-ORTHOPEDIC PATIENT on HEPARIN with thrombocytopenia + new thrombosis = high suspicion for HIT (1-5% incidence with UFH; post-orthopedic + female + age higher risk). RECOGNITION + 4Ts assessment + HIT antibody testing + multidisciplinary engagement. RN role: history + lab interpretation + escalation + family communication.

labs.v: HEPARIN-INDUCED THROMBOCYTOPENIA (HIT) TYPE II = IMMUNE-MEDIATED PROTHROMBOTIC syndrome (despite low platelets) caused by IgG antibodies against PLATELET FACTOR 4 (PF4)/HEPARIN COMPLEX → activates platelets via FcγRIIa receptor → platelet aggregation + microparticle release + endothelial activation + monocyte activation + tissue factor → THROMBOSIS (paradoxical given thrombocytopenia — counterintuitive but central to HIT); also platelet consumption + degranulation contributes to thrombocytopenia. INCIDENCE: ~1-5% with UNFRACTIONATED HEPARIN (UFH) + 0.1-1% with LOW-MOLECULAR-WEIGHT HEPARIN (LMWH — enoxaparin + dalteparin + tinzaparin; LMWH 5-10× less risk than UFH); essentially ZERO with FONDAPARINUX (synthetic factor Xa inhibitor; no PF4 cross-reactivity); RISK FACTORS = TYPE OF HEPARIN (UFH > LMWH > fondaparinux + bovine > porcine); DURATION (5-10 days typical); POPULATION (post-cardiac surgery 1-3% highest; post-orthopedic 0.5-2%; medical patients lower); SEX (female > male); AGE (elderly higher); CARDIAC SURGERY + CPB exposure higher; RECENT HEPARIN exposure within 100 days (rapid-onset HIT within 24 hr if re-exposure). TIMING typically 5-10 DAYS after heparin initiation (this patient POD 7 — typical window); RAPID-ONSET HIT within 24 hr in patients with recent heparin exposure (past 100 days; cross-reactivity); DELAYED-ONSET HIT after heparin stopped — up to 3 weeks (rare; antibodies persist + clinical deterioration). HIT TYPE I = NON-IMMUNE + transient direct heparin effect on platelets + within 1-4 days + mild (10-20% drop) + recovers without treatment + does not require alternative anticoagulant + heparin can be continued; HIT TYPE II = IMMUNE + serious + needs intervention + this patient. 4Ts SCORE = pre-test probability tool 0-8 points (Greinacher + Lo): (1) THROMBOCYTOPENIA SEVERITY — fall >50% with nadir ≥20k = 2 points; 30-50% fall OR nadir 10-19k = 1 point; <30% fall OR nadir <10k = 0; (2) TIMING — typical 5-10 days post heparin start = 2 points; <4 days with recent heparin exposure within past 100 days = 2 points; <4 days no recent exposure OR >10 days OR unclear = 0-1 point; (3) THROMBOSIS — new venous/arterial thrombosis confirmed = 2 points; skin necrosis at heparin injection sites = 2 points; suspected but not confirmed thrombosis = 1 point; no thrombosis = 0; (4) OTHER causes for thrombocytopenia — no other apparent cause = 2 points; possible alternative = 1 point; definite alternative cause = 0 points (sepsis + DIC + drug + ITP + bone marrow failure + heparin-coated catheter + post-transfusion); TOTAL SCORE 0-3 = LOW probability HIT (<5%); 4-5 = INTERMEDIATE (10-30%); 6-8 = HIGH (30-80%); HIGH 4Ts ALONE warrants empiric treatment + heparin discontinuation while awaiting confirmatory testing. THIS PATIENT 4Ts calculation: (1) Thrombocytopenia 60% fall + nadir 88 = 2 points; (2) Timing 7 days post heparin start = 2 points; (3) New DVT confirmed by Doppler = 2 points; (4) Other causes possible but heparin most likely + other causes assessed (no sepsis + no DIC + no medications other than oxycodone + no other concerning factors) = 1 point; TOTAL 4Ts = 7 (HIGH probability HIT) — empiric treatment indicated. LABORATORY CONFIRMATION: (1) ANTI-PF4 ELISA (immunoassay) — detects IgG/IgM/IgA antibodies to PF4/heparin complex; sensitive ~99% but not specific ~85% (many false positives — only IgG matters clinically + functional activity matters); positive if optical density (OD) >0.4 + strongly positive >2.0 + likely confirms HIT; (2) FUNCTIONAL ASSAY = SEROTONIN RELEASE ASSAY (SRA — gold standard + serotonin release from donor platelets exposed to patient serum + heparin) OR HEPARIN-INDUCED PLATELET AGGREGATION (HIPA — similar; less standardized); takes hours-days; more specific than ELISA + confirms clinical relevance of antibodies; positive ≥20% release of serotonin at therapeutic heparin concentration + low/no release at high heparin (heparin-dependent — characteristic of HIT); (3) WORKUP for other causes (sepsis + DIC + ITP + bone marrow failure + drug-induced + post-transfusion + heparin-coated devices) — assess for alternative explanations + treat accordingly; (4) IMAGING for thrombosis — Doppler ultrasound for DVT + CT chest if PE suspected + CT abdomen/pelvis if abdominal symptoms + cardiac imaging if cardiac source. SUSPECTED HIT requires IMMEDIATE ACTION while awaiting confirmation (don't wait for confirmation if 4Ts high — empiric treatment based on 4Ts).
labs.r: HIT TYPE II = immune-mediated prothrombotic syndrome; 4Ts SCORE 0-8 = pre-test probability; ≥6 high warrants empiric treatment; LABORATORY confirmation via anti-PF4 ELISA (sensitive + many false positives) + functional SRA/HIPA (specific gold standard). EMPIRIC TREATMENT based on 4Ts high — don't wait for laboratory confirmation if clinically suspected.

labs.n: HIT TREATMENT — STOP heparin + alternative anticoagulant + AVOID warfarin/platelets
labs.v: HIT MANAGEMENT — TIME-CRITICAL: (1) IMMEDIATELY STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS — UNFRACTIONATED HEPARIN (drips + flushes + line maintenance for arterial/central lines + heparin-coated catheters) + LMWH (enoxaparin + dalteparin + tinzaparin) + DANAPAROID (cross-reactivity); EVEN HEPARIN FLUSHES contraindicated — use SALINE FLUSHES for IV/arterial/central line maintenance; LIFETIME AVOIDANCE of heparin recommended for HIT patients (antibodies wane over months-years but can recur with re-exposure + may cause anaphylactic reaction in some); MEDICAL ALERT bracelet at discharge; (2) START ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT IMMEDIATELY even before HIT confirmation if 4Ts ≥4: (a) ARGATROBAN — DIRECT THROMBIN INHIBITOR (DTI) synthetic small molecule; IV continuous infusion 2 mcg/kg/min (0.5-1 mcg/kg/min in hepatic dysfunction or critical illness) titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline (typically 50-100 sec from baseline 30-40); RENAL CLEARANCE minimal (primarily HEPATIC clearance) — REDUCE DOSE in hepatic dysfunction + critical illness; half-life ~50 min; preferred for renal dysfunction; AFFECTS PT/INR (cannot guide warfarin transition by INR alone — use chromogenic factor Xa or anti-IIa for transition planning); HIGH-ALERT MEDICATION + 2-RN verification + barcode scanning; (b) BIVALIRUDIN — DIRECT THROMBIN INHIBITOR (DTI) peptide; IV continuous infusion 0.15-0.20 mg/kg/hr titrated to aPTT 1.5-2.5× baseline; shorter half-life ~25 min (preferred for CARDIAC SURGERY + procedures + need for rapid offset); RENAL CLEARANCE substantial — adjust for renal impairment; also affects PT/INR; (c) FONDAPARINUX — FACTOR Xa INHIBITOR synthetic + no PF4 cross-reactivity + can be used in STABLE HIT after acute treatment + outpatient; SC daily 7.5 mg (5 mg if weight <50 kg; 10 mg if >100 kg); not for acute HITT due to renal clearance + dosing constraints; (d) DOAC for STABLE HIT — RIVAROXABAN 15 mg BID × 21 days then 20 mg daily OR APIXABAN 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID OR DABIGATRAN 150 mg BID OR EDOXABAN 60 mg daily; increasingly used for HIT + outpatient + good safety + no INR monitoring; STABLE HIT acceptable + ARGATROBAN/BIVALIRUDIN still standard for ACUTE HITT (HIT with thrombosis); (3) AVOID WARFARIN until platelets recover >150k — early warfarin in HIT depletes PROTEIN C (vitamin K-dependent + short half-life ~8 hr) BEFORE depleting prothrombotic factors → transient hypercoagulable state → WARFARIN-INDUCED SKIN NECROSIS + venous limb gangrene + microvascular thrombosis; protein C deficiency relative; AVOID warfarin in acute HIT; if patient was on warfarin pre-HIT diagnosis = reverse with vitamin K + transition to non-warfarin alternative; transition to warfarin once platelets recover + overlap with argatroban × 5 days + INR target 2-3 then discontinue argatroban; DOAC alternative simpler (no overlap needed beyond 24-48 hr); (4) AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION (even with very low platelets) — counterintuitive but evidence-based; platelet transfusion in HIT can paradoxically WORSEN thrombosis by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation; only consider for LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare in HIT — HIT is prothrombotic not bleeding); GENERALLY CONTRAINDICATED; (5) IMAGING for thrombosis — Doppler ultrasound for DVT bilaterally; CT chest if PE suspected; CT abdomen/pelvis if abdominal symptoms; cardiac imaging if cardiac source; (6) DURATION of anticoagulation = AT LEAST 3 MONTHS for HIT with thrombosis (HITT) per ACCP + multidisciplinary; AT LEAST 4 WEEKS for ISOLATED HIT without thrombosis (HIT WITHOUT THROMBOSIS — increased thrombosis risk; anticoagulation reduces; some advocate 30% develop thrombosis in following weeks if untreated); DURATION varies by clinical situation + provoking factor + risk; (7) MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + documentation in chart + family + medical home + outpatient hematology + lifetime avoidance of heparin (even heparin flushes + heparin-coated catheters + dental anesthesia with heparinized saline + caution); (8) CARDIAC SURGERY in HIT patients = challenging — BIVALIRUDIN commonly used during CPB + informed consent + multidisciplinary; ARGATROBAN during cardiac surgery less commonly used; ARGATROBAN avoided in cardiac surgery + bivalirudin preferred; PLATELET HISTORY informs management. CONSEQUENCES of UNDETECTED HIT = paradoxical thrombosis (DVT + PE + arterial — stroke + MI + limb ischemia + skin necrosis at injection sites + adrenal hemorrhage rare); mortality 5-10% even with treatment + higher untreated; significant morbidity (limb loss + amputation + organ damage). FOLLOW-UP HIT testing — anti-PF4 antibodies typically wane within 100 days (median ~50 days) + functional assay clears first; routine retesting not necessary; if re-exposure unavoidable + appropriate clinical situation = monitor closely + alternative if antibodies persist. CULTURAL + family + emotional + spiritual support throughout + transparent communication.
labs.r: HIT MANAGEMENT = (1) STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS including flushes + LIFETIME avoidance; (2) START alternative anticoagulant = ARGATROBAN (DTI; preferred renal; hepatic clearance) OR BIVALIRUDIN (DTI; preferred cardiac surgery + procedures; renal clearance) OR FONDAPARINUX (Xa inhibitor; stable HIT + outpatient) OR DOAC (stable HIT + outpatient); (3) AVOID WARFARIN until platelets recover >150k (warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion); (4) AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION (paradoxically worsens thrombosis); (5) IMAGING for thrombosis; (6) DURATION 3+ months HITT + 4+ weeks isolated; (7) MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + documentation + lifetime avoidance.

labs.v: ARGATROBAN administration + monitoring + transition planning: PHARMACOLOGY = DIRECT THROMBIN INHIBITOR (DTI) synthetic small molecule that binds reversibly to active site of thrombin (free + clot-bound thrombin); no antidote (unlike heparin reversed with protamine); short half-life ~50 min (so discontinuation = rapid offset); primarily HEPATIC clearance (90%) + renal 10%; well-tolerated. INDICATIONS = (1) HIT or suspected HIT; (2) HIT-associated thrombosis; (3) percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) in HIT or at risk for HIT; INFUSION DOSING for HIT: 2 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion (initial dose for typical patient) titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline (typical target 50-100 sec from baseline 30-40); REDUCED DOSE 0.5-1.2 mcg/kg/min for hepatic dysfunction (Child-Pugh B/C) + critical illness (heart failure + anasarca + post-cardiac surgery + multiorgan dysfunction); FOR PCI = bolus 350 mcg/kg followed by infusion 25 mcg/kg/min titrated. MONITORING = aPTT q2-4h initial titration → q4-6h once stable; goal 1.5-3× baseline (typically 50-100 sec); CONTROL aPTT obtained baseline before initiation; ALT/AST monitoring (hepatic clearance) + INR monitoring (impacted by argatroban — increased baseline INR ~2-3 confounds warfarin transition); PT/INR cannot guide warfarin transition by INR alone — use CHROMOGENIC FACTOR Xa or ANTI-IIa OR target INR while ON argatroban + then discontinue argatroban to assess warfarin alone. SIDE EFFECTS = BLEEDING (similar to other anticoagulants — major bleeding 7-10% + intracranial 1%); NO ANTIDOTE — supportive care + cessation + plasma transfusion (no proven efficacy) + FFP + cryoprecipitate; ELEVATED LFTs (rare); ARRHYTHMIA + hypotension (rare). TRANSITION FROM ARGATROBAN TO ORAL ANTICOAGULATION: (1) WARFARIN transition — wait until platelets recover >150k + give warfarin while continuing argatroban × 5 days minimum overlap (warfarin takes 5+ days for full effect via vitamin K-dependent factor depletion); ARGATROBAN AFFECTS INR (artificially elevates) — assess INR PRE-warfarin to establish baseline + adjust target; some institutions use CHROMOGENIC FACTOR Xa or anti-IIa for argatroban + INR for warfarin separately; STOP argatroban + recheck INR 2 hr later — target INR 2-3 sustained on warfarin alone; continue warfarin × 3 months for HITT + 4 weeks for isolated HIT; (2) DOAC TRANSITION — APIXABAN 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID for acute DVT in HIT (this patient) OR rivaroxaban 15 mg BID × 21 days then 20 mg daily; DOACs have rapid onset (within hours) — less overlap needed than warfarin; STANDARD = start DOAC while on argatroban × 24-48 hr to ensure overlap then discontinue argatroban; SIMPLER + outpatient + no INR monitoring + no protein C depletion concern + comparable efficacy + similar bleeding risk; (3) FONDAPARINUX transition — synthetic factor Xa inhibitor; no PF4 cross-reactivity; SC daily; for STABLE HIT + outpatient. HIGH-ALERT MEDICATION = 2-RN verification + barcode scanning + smart pump hard limits + standardized order set + close monitoring + escalation if bleeding or abnormal aPTT. THIS PATIENT plan: ARGATROBAN 2 mcg/kg/min initial (no hepatic dysfunction) titrated to aPTT 78 sec (within 50-100 target); 4-6 hr aPTT monitoring; PLATELETS recovering 88 → 78 → 142 → 218 over 4 days; APIXABAN 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID chosen over warfarin (avoiding warfarin-induced skin necrosis + protein C depletion + simpler outpatient management); argatroban-apixaban overlap × 24 hr then discontinue argatroban; total anticoagulation duration ~3 months for HITT (right DVT confirmed).
labs.r: ARGATROBAN = direct thrombin inhibitor + synthetic + hepatic clearance + short half-life + no antidote; DOSE 2 mcg/kg/min (reduce for hepatic dysfunction); aPTT q2-6h target 50-100 sec; affects INR (cannot guide warfarin transition by INR); TRANSITION to warfarin (overlap × 5 days + after platelets recover) or DOAC (overlap × 24-48 hr; simpler + outpatient); HIGH-ALERT med + 2-RN verification + barcode + smart pump.

labs.n: WARFARIN AVOIDANCE in acute HIT + DOAC transition + secondary prevention
labs.v: WARFARIN AVOIDANCE in acute HIT is CRITICAL: (1) PATHOPHYSIOLOGY = warfarin inhibits vitamin K-dependent factor synthesis (factors II, VII, IX, X + proteins C + S); ANTICOAGULANT factors C + S have SHORTER half-lives (~8 hr for C) than prothrombotic factors II + VII + IX + X (longer half-lives 24-72 hr); EARLY warfarin in HIT (when patient already PROTHROMBOTIC from HIT) DEPLETES PROTEIN C + S FIRST → transient hypercoagulable state lasting hours-days → WARFARIN-INDUCED SKIN NECROSIS (microvascular thrombosis in subcutaneous fat + skin necrosis at extremities; rare but devastating) + VENOUS LIMB GANGRENE (massive limb thrombosis + ischemia + tissue loss + amputation risk) + MORTALITY; (2) AVOID warfarin until platelet count recovers ≥150k (typically 5-14 days after stopping heparin + appropriate alternative anticoagulant); PROTEIN C levels normalize when full anticoagulation established with non-warfarin agent + platelet recovery; (3) IF PATIENT WAS ON WARFARIN PRE-HIT diagnosis → reverse with VITAMIN K + transition to non-warfarin alternative (argatroban or bivalirudin) immediately; (4) TRANSITION to warfarin (when appropriate): platelets ≥150k + on argatroban + overlap × 5 days minimum + warfarin starting dose typical (5 mg daily) + monitor INR + ARGATROBAN affects INR artificially (elevates) — use chromogenic factor Xa or anti-IIa for argatroban + INR for warfarin separately + STOP argatroban + recheck INR 2 hr later — target INR 2-3 sustained on warfarin alone; continue warfarin × 3 months for HITT + 4 weeks for isolated HIT; (5) DOAC ALTERNATIVE (this patient — apixaban) = preferred over warfarin in many HIT patients due to: (a) no protein C depletion concern (DOACs do not affect protein C); (b) rapid onset (full anticoagulation within hours; less argatroban overlap needed); (c) no INR monitoring (simpler outpatient + medication adherence); (d) comparable efficacy + similar bleeding risk; (e) no significant drug interactions with most medications; (f) no dietary interactions (unlike warfarin + vitamin K foods); (6) DURATION = 3+ months for HIT with thrombosis (HITT) per ACCP guidelines; 4+ weeks for isolated HIT without thrombosis; longer if unprovoked or persistent risk factors; (7) FOLLOW-UP outpatient hematology + monitoring; recurrent HIT testing typically not needed (antibodies wane); medical alert bracelet + lifetime heparin avoidance; family + community + insurance + multidisciplinary; (8) ADHERENCE counseling + bleeding precautions + medical alert + dental + procedures + medication interactions + family education + emergency plan. THIS PATIENT: APIXABAN 10 mg PO BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID chosen for: (a) acute DVT treatment standard dose; (b) avoid warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene; (c) no protein C depletion concern; (d) simpler outpatient management + no INR monitoring; (e) preferred DOAC for orthopedic surgery patient (vs rivaroxaban 15 mg BID × 21 days then 20 mg daily acceptable alternative); (f) family + adherence excellent + supportive; (g) Mediterranean diet + lifestyle continued; ARGATROBAN-APIXABAN OVERLAP × 24-48 hr to ensure transition + then discontinue argatroban + outpatient apixaban × 3 months total duration for HITT. MEDICAL ALERT bracelet ordered + lifetime heparin avoidance documented + outpatient hematology + orthopedic follow-up + PCP + community + family education.
labs.r: WARFARIN AVOIDANCE in acute HIT critical (warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion); transition to warfarin or DOAC after platelets recover ≥150k + appropriate alternative anticoagulant + appropriate overlap. DOACs preferred over warfarin in many HIT patients (no protein C depletion + rapid onset + simpler outpatient).

labs.v: PLATELET TRANSFUSION AVOIDANCE in HIT is COUNTERINTUITIVE but evidence-based: (1) HIT IS PROTHROMBOTIC despite low platelets — patient is at HIGH RISK FOR THROMBOSIS (30-75% develop thrombosis without treatment) NOT BLEEDING; bleeding is rare in HIT (the thrombosis is the main concern); (2) PLATELET TRANSFUSION in HIT can PARADOXICALLY WORSEN THROMBOSIS by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation + extending existing thrombosis or precipitating new thrombosis; multiple case reports of platelet transfusion in HIT precipitating PE + DVT + arterial thrombosis + worsening outcomes; (3) GENERAL CONTRAINDICATION to platelet transfusion in HIT EVEN WITH VERY LOW PLATELET COUNTS; (4) EXCEPTIONS = LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare in HIT) — only situation where platelet transfusion may be considered + multidisciplinary discussion + careful risk-benefit + escalation to hematology; (5) ALTERNATIVE BLEEDING MANAGEMENT in HIT — control bleeding source + supportive care + appropriate anticoagulant reversal (no proven antidote for argatroban — supportive care + FFP/cryoprecipitate may help); (6) BLEEDING PRECAUTIONS during HIT treatment — bleeding risk on argatroban similar to other anticoagulants; monitor for bleeding signs (hematemesis + melena + hematuria + nosebleeds + hematomas + skin bruising); reduce/discontinue argatroban if bleeding + reverse with FFP + supportive care; (7) PROCEDURES + invasive procedures in HIT — delay non-urgent procedures until platelet recovery + INR/aPTT acceptable; HIT patients can have procedures with appropriate anticoagulant management + bridging + multidisciplinary; (8) MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + LIFETIME heparin avoidance + future medical encounters including dental + procedures + emergency situations + family + community awareness. THIS PATIENT: NO platelet transfusion despite low platelets 88 at admission (counterintuitive but correct — patient is prothrombotic + HIT is treated with anticoagulation not platelet transfusion); MONITOR for any bleeding signs (rare in HIT); argatroban titrated to aPTT 50-100 sec + close monitoring; no procedures planned during acute phase; PLATELETS recovered 88 → 78 → 142 → 218 over 4 days (typical pattern as antibodies wane); ANTICOAGULATION maintained throughout per HIT management. CULTURAL + family + spiritual + emotional support throughout (Chinese-American + bilingual home + Buddhist + chaplain coordinated with Buddhist chaplain).
labs.r: PLATELET TRANSFUSION AVOIDANCE in HIT is critical + counterintuitive but evidence-based — paradoxically worsens thrombosis. Exception = life-threatening bleeding (rare). Bleeding precautions on argatroban + appropriate management. MONITOR for thrombosis + bleeding throughout. RN role: education + advocacy + monitoring + family education.

labs.v: PERTINENT NEGATIVES: NO pulmonary embolism (no chest pain + no SOB + no tachycardia + no hypoxemia + no signs of PE); NO arterial thrombosis (no stroke + no MI + no limb ischemia + no skin necrosis at heparin injection sites); NO bleeding complications (no hematuria + no melena + no nosebleeds + no hematomas + no excessive bruising; rare in HIT); NO sepsis (no fever beyond mild + no concerning signs + no positive cultures); NO DIC (coags normal + fibrinogen preserved + no schistocytes + no organ dysfunction beyond DVT); NO ITP (no other cause for thrombocytopenia identified — heparin is most likely + 4Ts high + HIT testing positive); NO adrenal hemorrhage (no abdominal pain + no hypotension + no electrolyte abnormality); NO concurrent active malignancy (remote breast cancer in remission 8 yr ago — annual surveillance + no current malignancy); NO platelet transfusion needed (HIT prothrombotic + AVOID platelet transfusion); NO warfarin initiation acutely (avoiding warfarin-induced skin necrosis + protein C depletion); NO hepatic dysfunction (LFTs normal — appropriate argatroban dosing without adjustment); NO renal dysfunction (Cr 0.9 normal — minimal argatroban renal clearance + appropriate dosing); insurance + multidisciplinary team available + experienced; family supportive + receptive + bilingual + cultural sensitivity. PROTECTIVE FACTORS: RAPID RECOGNITION at POD 7 routine labs + platelet decline pattern + new DVT trigger workup; 4Ts SCORE 7 (HIGH) prompted immediate empiric treatment + multidisciplinary engagement; ANTIBODY TESTING POSITIVE confirming HIT diagnosis (anti-PF4 ELISA + functional SRA both positive); ARGATROBAN INITIATED IMMEDIATELY at recognition + appropriate dosing + aPTT monitoring + therapeutic range; HEPARIN STOPPED COMPLETELY (including flushes) + saline flushes substituted + lifetime heparin avoidance planned; AVOIDED WARFARIN initially (preventing warfarin-induced skin necrosis); AVOIDED PLATELET TRANSFUSION despite low platelets (paradoxical worsening prevention); DOAC TRANSITION to APIXABAN (preferred over warfarin in HIT + simpler outpatient + no INR + no protein C depletion); PATIENT RESPONSIVE to argatroban (platelets recovered + DVT improving + no progression + no new thrombosis); FAMILY supportive + extended family available + insurance excellent + multidisciplinary outpatient care accessible; CULTURAL + SPIRITUAL support (Chinese-American + Buddhist + chaplain) + family integration + interpreter services if needed (English-fluent here); MEDICAL ALERT bracelet ordered + lifetime heparin avoidance documented + medication reconciliation + outpatient hematology follow-up; PATIENT receptive + motivated + grateful + recovering well; HIP REPLACEMENT recovery on track despite HIT complication. STANDARD expected positive trajectory with comprehensive intervention + recovery from both HIT + hip replacement + lifetime heparin avoidance for prevention.
labs.r: Despite serious HIT complication, multiple PROTECTIVE FACTORS supported recovery: rapid recognition + 4Ts assessment + empiric treatment + appropriate alternative anticoagulant + warfarin avoidance + platelet transfusion avoidance + DOAC transition + multidisciplinary + family + insurance + cultural sensitivity. Standard expected positive trajectory with comprehensive intervention + lifetime heparin avoidance.

nursesNote: POD 7 0700 — Routine labs revealed PLATELET DROP from 220 (preop baseline) → 158 (POD 4) → 112 (POD 6) → 88 (POD 7 = 60% DROP); patient also complaining of NEW RIGHT LOWER EXTREMITY pain + swelling + warmth × 8 hr overnight onset. PATIENT: 65 y/o female M. Chen, POD 7 from elective RIGHT TOTAL HIP ARTHROPLASTY (THA) for severe osteoarthritis. PMH: HTN (lisinopril + amlodipine controlled), hyperlipidemia (atorvastatin 40 mg), T2DM (metformin 1000 BID + HbA1c 6.8), obesity (BMI 28), severe OA right hip + chronic LBP, remote breast cancer 8 yr ago (stage 1 mastectomy + adjuvant chemo + 5-year tamoxifen completed; in remission + annual surveillance). SURGICAL HX: appendectomy childhood + mastectomy + reconstruction 8 yr ago + right TKA 6 yr ago (recovered well + no complications + standard perioperative heparin × prior surgeries without prior reactions; recent heparin exposure unknown >100 days) + right THA 7 days ago (current admission). DVT PROPHYLAXIS: SUBCUTANEOUS HEPARIN 5000 units q8h SC × 7 days since POD 0 (per institutional protocol; some institutions use enoxaparin). POD 1-5 progressed well — PT + early mobility + tolerating diet + minimal pain + planned discharge POD 6 or 7. POD 7 0700 AM LABS: PLATELETS 88 (60% drop from baseline 220); Hgb 11.2 + WBC 8.4 + normal differential; PT 13.4 + INR 1.1 (normal); aPTT 32 (normal — pretreatment baseline); fibrinogen 380; LFTs normal; Cr 0.9 (normal); BUN 14; electrolytes normal. CLINICAL EXAM at 0700: HR 88 NSR + BP 138/82 + RR 16 + SpO2 97% RA + T 37.0 + alert + oriented + appropriate + weight 68 kg; NEW RIGHT LOWER EXTREMITY pain + swelling + warmth × 8 hr; right calf circumference 38 cm vs left 34 cm (4 cm difference; significant); Homan's sign positive (calf pain on dorsiflexion); pitting edema 2+ right; warmth + erythema; HIP surgical site healing well + no concerning signs. STAT DOPPLER ULTRASOUND right lower extremity at 0830 = ACUTE DVT in right common femoral vein + extending to popliteal vein (no extension above femoral); no left lower extremity DVT. 4Ts SCORE CALCULATION (Greinacher + Lo pre-test probability tool) by RN: (1) THROMBOCYTOPENIA SEVERITY = 60% fall + nadir 88 = 2 POINTS; (2) TIMING = 7 days post heparin start (within typical 5-10 day window for HIT type II) = 2 POINTS; (3) THROMBOSIS = NEW DVT confirmed by Doppler = 2 POINTS; (4) OTHER CAUSES = hospitalized post-op + heparin most likely + other causes thoroughly assessed (no sepsis + no DIC + no other medications + no malignancy clinically + no other concerning factors) = 1 POINT; TOTAL 4Ts = 7 (HIGH PROBABILITY HIT — 30-80% confirmation rate; empiric treatment indicated without waiting for laboratory confirmation). HIT ANTIBODIES STAT sent: anti-PF4 ELISA + functional SRA. HEMATOLOGY consultation activated; multidisciplinary engagement (orthopedic surgeon + hematologist + ICU intensivist + multidisciplinary). STAT DECISION at 0930: STOP ALL HEPARIN + INITIATE ARGATROBAN. POD 7 0930-1000: ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS STOPPED — subcutaneous heparin DVT prophylaxis DISCONTINUED + heparin flushes for IV access REMOVED + replaced with NORMAL SALINE FLUSHES + heparin-bonded catheters not present + no other heparin sources; PROVIDER ORDER: NO HEPARIN OR HEPARIN-CONTAINING PRODUCTS in any form (LIFETIME MEDICAL ALERT) + place medical alert bracelet at discharge. ARGATROBAN 2 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion initiated at 1000 (68 kg × 2 = 136 mcg/min = 8.2 mg/hr); titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline (target 50-100 sec from baseline 32); HIGH-ALERT MEDICATION + 2-RN verification + barcode scanning + smart pump hard limits + standardized order set + close monitoring; BASELINE aPTT obtained pre-argatroban; q2-4h aPTT monitoring during titration; renal dose adjustment unnecessary (Cr 0.9 normal); CAUTION HEPATIC dysfunction (argatroban primarily hepatic clearance) — LFTs normal so no adjustment needed; PT/INR will be affected by argatroban (cannot guide warfarin transition by INR alone — use chromogenic factor Xa or anti-IIa for transition planning); FAMILY meeting + transparent communication + Chinese-American family + Buddhist chaplain + emotional support + multidisciplinary engagement + cultural sensitivity. POD 7 1400 (4 hr into argatroban): aPTT 78 sec (target 50-100 sec = 1.5-3× baseline 32; within therapeutic range); argatroban dose maintained at 2 mcg/kg/min; continued q4-6h aPTT monitoring. AVOIDED WARFARIN (warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion if started early in HIT). AVOIDED PLATELET TRANSFUSION despite low platelets (counterintuitive — paradoxical worsening of thrombosis in HIT). IMAGING surveillance for new thrombosis (chest pain → CT chest for PE; abdominal symptoms → CT abdomen if concerns) — none needed. DAY 2 morning 0700 (24 hr into argatroban): aPTT 76 sec therapeutic; argatroban maintained; PLATELETS 78 (still slightly declining — expected; will rebound when antibodies clear over days); LABS BACK: ANTI-PF4 ELISA POSITIVE (OD 1.8 — strongly positive; threshold >0.4); FUNCTIONAL SRA POSITIVE — HIT CONFIRMED LABORATORY; right leg DVT swelling/pain decreased with argatroban + leg elevation; no new thrombosis on clinical exam + no signs of PE; orthopedic + hematology + multidisciplinary discussion = continue argatroban × at least 5 days + plan for warfarin overlap when platelets recover OR DOAC transition (simpler + outpatient + no INR monitoring + no protein C depletion + comparable efficacy). steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters at this HIT recognition?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: HEPARIN-INDUCED THROMBOCYTOPENIA (HIT) Type II = immune-mediated PROTHROMBOTIC syndrome (despite low platelets) caused by IgG antibodies against PF4/heparin complex → activates platelets + thrombosis; 30-75% develop thrombosis without treatment + 5-10% mortality; incidence ~1-5% with UFH + 0.1-1% with LMWH; this patient on UFH 5000 units q8h × 7 days = at risk; timing 5-10 days typical (this patient POD 7); risk factors = post-orthopedic + female + age
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — HIT pathophysiology + epidemiology + risk factors. Foundation for recognition + treatment. RN role: recognition + escalation + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: 4Ts SCORE pre-test probability (0-8 points): (1) THROMBOCYTOPENIA SEVERITY — fall >50% with nadir ≥20k = 2 points; (2) TIMING — typical 5-10 days = 2 points; (3) THROMBOSIS — new venous/arterial = 2 points; (4) OTHER CAUSES — none apparent = 2 points; 0-3 LOW + 4-5 INTERMEDIATE + 6-8 HIGH; this patient 4Ts = 7 (HIGH probability HIT) — empiric treatment indicated without waiting for laboratory confirmation
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — 4Ts pre-test probability tool. Standard practice. RN role: assessment + escalation.

opts.text: STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS IMMEDIATELY at HIT recognition — UFH (drips + flushes + line maintenance) + LMWH (enoxaparin + dalteparin) + heparin-coated catheters + heparin-bonded devices; EVEN HEPARIN FLUSHES contraindicated — use SALINE flushes; LIFETIME HEPARIN AVOIDANCE (antibodies wane but may recur with re-exposure + medical alert bracelet)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — heparin discontinuation including flushes + lifetime avoidance. RN role: ensure heparin completely stopped + saline flushes + medical alert + documentation.

opts.text: START ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT immediately even before HIT confirmation if 4Ts ≥4: ARGATROBAN (DIRECT THROMBIN INHIBITOR — IV continuous 2 mcg/kg/min titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline; primarily hepatic clearance + caution in liver disease + half-life 50 min) OR BIVALIRUDIN (DTI; renal clearance + preferred for cardiac surgery) OR FONDAPARINUX (factor Xa inhibitor; stable HIT + outpatient) OR DOAC (apixaban + rivaroxaban + dabigatran for stable HIT)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — alternative anticoagulant initiation + selection. RN role: medication administration + monitoring + escalation.

opts.text: AVOID WARFARIN until platelet count recovers ≥150k — warfarin-induced SKIN NECROSIS + venous limb gangrene from PROTEIN C depletion (protein C half-life 8 hr; depleted before prothrombotic factors → transient hypercoagulable state); transition to warfarin once platelets recover + overlap with argatroban × 5 days; DOAC alternative simpler + preferred in many HIT patients (no protein C depletion + rapid onset + no INR monitoring + comparable efficacy)
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — warfarin avoidance in acute HIT. Standard practice. RN role: medication management + advocacy + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION even with very low platelets — counterintuitive but evidence-based; platelet transfusion in HIT paradoxically WORSENS thrombosis by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation; only consider for LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare in HIT — HIT is PROTHROMBOTIC not bleeding); standard contraindication
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — platelet transfusion avoidance. Counterintuitive but standard practice. RN role: advocacy + education + monitoring.

opts.text: LABORATORY CONFIRMATION via ANTI-PF4 ELISA (sensitive ~99% + not specific ~85% — many false positives; positive OD >0.4 + strongly positive >2.0) + FUNCTIONAL SRA/HIPA (specific gold standard + clinically relevant antibodies; takes hours-days); EMPIRIC TREATMENT based on 4Ts high — don't wait for laboratory confirmation; multidisciplinary engagement
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — laboratory confirmation + empiric treatment. Standard practice. RN role: lab coordination + escalation.

opts.text: DURATION of anticoagulation = at least 3 MONTHS for HIT WITH THROMBOSIS (HITT — this patient) + at least 4 WEEKS for isolated HIT WITHOUT THROMBOSIS; transition to outpatient DOAC (apixaban 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID) preferred over warfarin in HIT due to no antibody cross-reactivity + simpler outpatient management + no INR monitoring + no protein C depletion concern + comparable efficacy + similar bleeding risk
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — duration + DOAC transition for outpatient. RN role: education + multidisciplinary + outpatient coordination.

opts.text: MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + LIFETIME heparin avoidance documentation in chart + family + medical home + outpatient hematology + cultural + spiritual support + family education + adherence + bleeding monitoring + community resources + emergency contacts + lifelong management
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — discharge planning + lifelong management + medical alert. Foundation for long-term safety + recurrence prevention. RN role: documentation + education + advocacy.

opts.text: Allergies: NKDA — document and proceed
opts.rat: Allergy documentation. No medication contraindications. Not the priority cue but important for safety.

opts.text: CONTINUE SUBCUTANEOUS HEPARIN 5000 units q8h for DVT prophylaxis + monitor platelets; the platelet drop is likely post-operative consumption + new DVT is unrelated; HIT is rare + unlikely
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS approach. Continuing heparin in HIT IMMEDIATELY worsens thrombosis + can be fatal. 4Ts SCORE 7 (HIGH probability) + new thrombosis + appropriate timing + heparin exposure = HIT very likely + empiric treatment indicated. STANDARD = STOP ALL HEPARIN + start alternative anticoagulant + don't wait for confirmation if 4Ts high.

opts.text: Transfuse 1 unit of platelets for thrombocytopenia (platelets 88 — concerning); platelet transfusion will correct platelet count + reduce bleeding risk + improve clinical status
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS approach. HIT is PROTHROMBOTIC + not bleeding diathesis; PLATELET TRANSFUSION paradoxically WORSENS thrombosis by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation. STANDARD = AVOID platelet transfusion in HIT; only consider for LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare). The thrombocytopenia in HIT does NOT need correction with transfusion.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What HIT + alternative anticoagulant + warfarin principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: HIT TYPE II PATHOPHYSIOLOGY = IgG antibodies against PF4/heparin complex → platelet activation via FcγRIIa receptor → aggregation + microparticle release + endothelial activation + monocyte activation + tissue factor → THROMBOSIS (paradoxical given thrombocytopenia); 30-75% develop thrombosis untreated + 5-10% mortality; PROTHROMBOTIC despite low platelets
opts.rat: CORRECT — HIT pathophysiology framework. Standard knowledge. Foundation for recognition + treatment.

opts.text: 4Ts SCORE pre-test probability (Greinacher + Lo) 0-8 points; ≥6 HIGH probability (30-80%) — empiric treatment indicated; LABORATORY CONFIRMATION via anti-PF4 ELISA (sensitive + many false positives) + functional SRA/HIPA (specific gold standard); EMPIRIC TREATMENT based on 4Ts high — don't wait for laboratory confirmation
opts.rat: CORRECT — 4Ts pre-test probability + laboratory framework. Standard practice. Foundation for empiric treatment.

opts.text: MANAGEMENT — (1) STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS immediately including flushes + LMWH + heparin-bonded catheters + LIFETIME avoidance; (2) START ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT immediately even before confirmation if 4Ts ≥4 — ARGATROBAN (DTI; hepatic) OR BIVALIRUDIN (DTI; cardiac surgery) OR FONDAPARINUX (Xa inhibitor; stable HIT outpatient) OR DOAC (stable HIT outpatient); (3) AVOID WARFARIN until platelets recover ≥150k (warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion); (4) AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION (paradoxical worsening — counterintuitive); (5) IMAGING for thrombosis; (6) DURATION 3+ months HITT + 4+ weeks isolated; (7) MEDICAL ALERT bracelet + LIFETIME avoidance
opts.rat: CORRECT — HIT management framework. Standard practice. Foundation for treatment + recurrence prevention.

opts.text: ARGATROBAN = direct thrombin inhibitor (DTI) synthetic; IV continuous 2 mcg/kg/min titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline (target 50-100 sec); primarily hepatic clearance (90%) + minimal renal (10%) — preferred for renal dysfunction; half-life ~50 min; no antidote; affects PT/INR (cannot guide warfarin transition by INR alone — use chromogenic factor Xa or anti-IIa); HIGH-ALERT medication + 2-RN verification + barcode + smart pump
opts.rat: CORRECT — argatroban pharmacology framework. Standard practice. RN role: medication administration + monitoring.

opts.text: WARFARIN AVOIDANCE in acute HIT — warfarin depletes vitamin K-dependent factors (II + VII + IX + X) + ANTICOAGULANT PROTEINS C + S; PROTEIN C HALF-LIFE 8 HR (shorter than prothrombotic factors); EARLY warfarin in HIT depletes protein C first → transient hypercoagulable state → WARFARIN-INDUCED SKIN NECROSIS + VENOUS LIMB GANGRENE; AVOID warfarin until platelets recover ≥150k + appropriate alternative anticoagulant + protein C levels normalize; DOAC alternative preferred (no protein C depletion + rapid onset + simpler outpatient)
opts.rat: CORRECT — warfarin avoidance framework + protein C mechanism. Standard practice. RN role: medication management + advocacy.

opts.text: PLATELET TRANSFUSION AVOIDANCE in HIT — counterintuitive but evidence-based; HIT is PROTHROMBOTIC + bleeding rare; platelet transfusion paradoxically WORSENS thrombosis by providing more substrate for activation + clot formation + extending existing thrombosis or precipitating new; multiple case reports of platelet transfusion in HIT precipitating PE + DVT + arterial thrombosis; GENERAL CONTRAINDICATION; EXCEPTION = LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare)
opts.rat: CORRECT — platelet transfusion avoidance framework. Counterintuitive but standard practice. Foundation for HIT safety.

opts.text: DOAC TRANSITION for STABLE HIT — APIXABAN 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID OR rivaroxaban 15 mg BID × 21 days then 20 mg daily OR dabigatran 150 mg BID OR edoxaban 60 mg daily; preferred over warfarin in HIT due to no antibody cross-reactivity + rapid onset (within hours; less argatroban overlap needed than warfarin's 5-day requirement) + no INR monitoring + no protein C depletion concern + comparable efficacy + similar bleeding risk + simpler outpatient management; 3+ months duration for HITT
opts.rat: CORRECT — DOAC transition framework. Standard practice. RN role: medication transition + education + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: CONTINUE SUBCUTANEOUS HEPARIN 5000 units q8h for DVT prophylaxis + monitor platelets; the platelet drop is likely post-operative consumption + new DVT is unrelated to heparin
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS. Continuing heparin in HIT IMMEDIATELY worsens thrombosis + can be fatal. 4Ts 7 (HIGH) + new thrombosis + appropriate timing = HIT likely + empiric treatment indicated.

opts.text: Transfuse 1 unit of platelets for thrombocytopenia + initiate warfarin 5 mg daily for DVT treatment + continue heparin for prophylaxis
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + MULTIPLE ERRORS. (1) AVOID platelet transfusion in HIT (paradoxical worsening); (2) AVOID warfarin in acute HIT until platelets recover (warfarin-induced skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion); (3) STOP all heparin immediately. Standard practice = STOP heparin + start argatroban/bivalirudin/fondaparinux/DOAC + AVOID warfarin + AVOID platelets.

opts.text: Wait for HIT antibody testing confirmation before starting alternative anticoagulant — empiric treatment based on 4Ts alone is premature + risks unnecessary anticoagulation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. EMPIRIC TREATMENT based on 4Ts ≥4 is STANDARD — laboratory confirmation takes hours-days + delaying treatment increases thrombosis risk + mortality. 4Ts 7 HIGH probability (30-80%) warrants immediate treatment. Standard practice = empiric treatment + confirm with labs.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns at this HIT presentation
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS + START ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT r/t 4Ts 7 (HIGH probability HIT) + new DVT + platelet 60% drop; STOP heparin including flushes (saline substitution) + ARGATROBAN 2 mcg/kg/min IV continuous titrated to aPTT 50-100 sec; HIGH-ALERT med + 2-RN verification + monitoring + escalation
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. STOP heparin + start alternative anticoagulant time-critical. Each minute of continued heparin worsens thrombosis. RN role: rapid medication administration + monitoring.

opts.text: AVOID WARFARIN + AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION r/t HIT acute management; warfarin causes skin necrosis + venous limb gangrene from protein C depletion until platelets recover ≥150k; platelet transfusion paradoxically worsens thrombosis; DOAC (apixaban) preferred over warfarin in HIT for outpatient
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Two critical avoidances. Standard practice. RN role: advocacy + education + escalation.

opts.text: HIT CONFIRMATION + thrombosis surveillance r/t anti-PF4 ELISA + functional SRA — laboratory confirmation; Doppler ultrasound bilateral DVT; CT chest if PE suspected; CT abdomen/pelvis if abdominal symptoms; ongoing imaging surveillance for new thrombosis throughout HIT management
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Confirmation + surveillance + multidisciplinary engagement. Foundation for diagnosis + treatment monitoring.

opts.text: DOAC TRANSITION + DISCHARGE PLANNING r/t apixaban 10 mg BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID for acute DVT in HIT; argatroban-apixaban overlap × 24-48 hr; 3+ months duration for HITT; medical alert bracelet + LIFETIME heparin avoidance + outpatient hematology + family education + adherence + bleeding precautions + community resources
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Transition + discharge + lifelong management. Foundation for outpatient + secondary prevention.

opts.text: FAMILY-CENTERED + multidisciplinary support + cultural sensitivity r/t husband + 2 adult daughters + 4 grandchildren + extended family + Buddhist family + chaplain coordinated + Chinese-American + bilingual home + English-fluent + interpreter not needed but offered; multidisciplinary engagement (hematology + orthopedic + ICU + pharmacy + chaplain + social worker + PCP)
opts.rat: #5 — Important throughout + at discharge. Family + multidisciplinary support + cultural sensitivity. Foundation for sustained recovery + family well-being.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this HIT.

opts.text: STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS IMMEDIATELY (UFH + LMWH + heparin flushes + heparin-bonded catheters) + substitute SALINE flushes; LIFETIME HEPARIN AVOIDANCE + medical alert bracelet at discharge + documentation + chart + future medical encounters
opts.rat: Standard HIT management. Time-critical. RN role: ensure complete heparin discontinuation + saline flushes + documentation + lifelong avoidance.

opts.text: INITIATE ALTERNATIVE NON-HEPARIN ANTICOAGULANT: ARGATROBAN 2 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion titrated to aPTT 50-100 sec (1.5-3× baseline); renal-dose if needed (this patient Cr 0.9 normal — no adjustment); hepatic-dose if needed (this patient LFTs normal — no adjustment); HIGH-ALERT medication + 2-RN verification + barcode + smart pump + close monitoring
opts.rat: Standard alternative anticoagulant initiation for HIT. RN role: medication preparation + administration + monitoring + escalation.

opts.rat: Standard warfarin avoidance in acute HIT. RN role: medication advocacy + education + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION despite low platelets — counterintuitive but evidence-based; platelet transfusion paradoxically WORSENS thrombosis in HIT; HIT is PROTHROMBOTIC NOT bleeding; reserved only for LIFE-THREATENING BLEEDING (rare in HIT)
opts.rat: Standard platelet transfusion avoidance in HIT. Counterintuitive but evidence-based. RN role: advocacy + education + monitoring.

opts.rat: Standard DOAC transition for stable HIT + outpatient management. Preferred over warfarin. RN role: medication transition + education + multidisciplinary.

opts.text: MEDICAL ALERT BRACELET + LIFETIME heparin avoidance + chart documentation + outpatient hematology follow-up + orthopedic outpatient + PCP medical home + family education + adherence + bleeding precautions + community resources + emergency contacts + lifelong management

opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS. Continuing heparin in HIT immediately worsens thrombosis + can be fatal. 4Ts 7 HIGH + new thrombosis + appropriate timing = HIT likely. STANDARD = STOP ALL HEPARIN IMMEDIATELY + start alternative anticoagulant.

opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. AVOID PLATELET TRANSFUSION in HIT — paradoxically worsens thrombosis. HIT is PROTHROMBOTIC NOT bleeding. Only consider for life-threatening bleeding (rare).

Wait for platelet recovery + appropriate alternative anticoagulant + protein C levels normalize. DOAC alternative preferred (no protein C depletion + simpler outpatient).

opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. EMPIRIC TREATMENT based on 4Ts ≥4 is STANDARD — laboratory confirmation takes hours-days + delaying treatment increases thrombosis risk + mortality. 4Ts 7 HIGH probability warrants immediate treatment.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence at HIT recognition
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: CALCULATE 4Ts SCORE + notify hematology STAT + STOP ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS (including flushes — substitute saline) + obtain baseline aPTT + LFTs + Cr + platelets + draw HIT antibody testing (anti-PF4 ELISA + functional SRA) + multidisciplinary engagement
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. 4Ts assessment + STOP heparin + labs + multidisciplinary. Time-critical. RN role: rapid + coordinated.

opts.text: INITIATE ARGATROBAN 2 mcg/kg/min IV continuous infusion (68 kg × 2 = 136 mcg/min) titrated to aPTT 1.5-3× baseline (50-100 sec); HIGH-ALERT medication + 2-RN verification + barcode + smart pump; q2-4h aPTT monitoring during titration → q4-6h once stable; renal/hepatic dose adjustment if needed
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Alternative anticoagulant initiation. Time-critical. RN role: medication administration + monitoring.

opts.text: IMAGING surveillance for thrombosis — Doppler ultrasound bilateral lower extremities; CT chest if PE suspected; CT abdomen if abdominal symptoms; AVOID warfarin until platelets recover ≥150k + AVOID platelet transfusion (paradoxical worsening)
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Thrombosis surveillance + critical avoidances. RN role: monitor + escalate + advocate.

opts.text: TRANSITION to OUTPATIENT DOAC (APIXABAN 10 mg PO BID × 7 days then 5 mg BID) once platelets recover ≥150k; argatroban-apixaban overlap × 24-48 hr then discontinue argatroban; 3+ months duration for HITT; outpatient hematology + medical alert bracelet + lifetime heparin avoidance + family education
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. DOAC transition + outpatient + recurrence prevention. Foundation for sustained management.

opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Family + multidisciplinary + comprehensive discharge + lifelong management. Foundation for sustained recovery.

opts.text: POD 7 1400 (4 hr into argatroban): aPTT 78 sec (target 50-100 sec); argatroban dose maintained 2 mcg/kg/min; ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS STOPPED including flushes; HIT antibody testing pending; right DVT swelling/pain improving with anticoagulation; no new thrombosis or bleeding; family supportive + Buddhist chaplain coordinated; multidisciplinary engagement

opts.rat: Standard expected Day 2 trajectory + laboratory confirmation + DVT improvement + multidisciplinary engagement. Foundation for ongoing care + transition planning.
